title: Quick Ligation Protocol (M2200)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a5jin","text":"This protocols is to be performed with the Quick Ligation Reaction Buffer. Please see the NEB website for more information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1ean7","text":"Thaw and resuspend Quick Ligase Reaction Buffer at  .","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":53}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":51,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"A8976D009DE111EAB603B5D7D7368055","id":0,"label":"","temperature":"","unit":32}}}}

steps: Set up the following reaction in a microcentrifuge tube On ice . Quick Ligase should be added last. Note that the table shows a ligation using a molar ratio of 1:3 vector to insert for the indicated DNA sizes. (Use NEBioCalculator to calculate molar ratios.) %s %s COMPONENT 20 μl REACTION Quick Ligase Reaction Buffer (2X)* 10 µl Vector DNA (4 kb) 50 ng (0.020 pmol) Insert DNA (1 kb) 37.5 ng (0.060 pmol) Nuclease-free Water to 20 µl Quick Ligase 1 µl
Gently mix the reaction by pipetting up and down and microfuge briefly.

Chill On ice and transform 1 µL – 5 µL of the reaction into 50 µL competent cells. Alternatively, Store at -20 °C . Do not heat inactivate – heat inactivation dramatically reduces transformation efficiency.
